rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend X-Complt; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT LINBECK GROUP, LLC MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend Cross-Complaint. Granted. Within 10 days, Linbeck Group LLC shall file its First Amended Cross-Complaint. The only opposition to the motion , filed by American Terrazzo, does not provide a basis to deny the motion. The trial is now set for February, 2014. American Terrazzo has not demonstrated prejudice to warrant denial. Linbeck Group LLC does not seek leave to add new parties to this action. The requested relief is not precluded by the Court's order dated January 14, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DINO JOHN BALDINI VS. AUTO RESCUE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled as to the negligence cause of action. The cause of action relates back to the original filing of the complaint. The Court sustains the demurrer to the fraudulent misrepresentation cause of action. That claim does not relate back and plaintiff needs to plead facts regarding his discovery of the fraud. The Court rejects defendant's remaining arguments. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DINO JOHN BALDINI VS. AUTO RESCUE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion to quash is denied. Plaintiff did not learn of the facts showing that Mr. Aguilar could be liable until plaintiff's investigator interviewed the eyewitness in December 2012. Plaintiff demonstrates ignorance within the meaning of CCP 474 and the Doe amendment was proper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BEVERLY BERNELL MYRES VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Physical Examination Of Pltf And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY Motion To Compel Physical Examination Of Plaintiff And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff. Granted, for good cause shown. Plaintiff has alleged "physical harm," and "serious harm to her already injured knee." (See, e.g., Complaint at 17, 25.) Plaintiff's physical condition is a basis for this action. The requested physical examination, limited in scope to approximately one hour, outweighs Plaintiff's privacy interest and is necessary for a fair resolution of the lawsuit. The examination must occur within 10 days, unless the parties mutually agree otherwise. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP A NEW VS. JOAN MACK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Forprotective Order And Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 10, PLAINTIFF KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP Motion For protective Order And Sanctions. Granted. As to the Third Set of Special Interrogatories: 52 are duplicative of previously propounded interrogatories. The deadline for filing a motion to compel has long passed. Defendant may not re-ask the same interrogatories. Professional Career Colleges, Magna Inst. v. Superior Court, 207 Cal. App. 3d 490, 493-494 (1989). As to the remaining four interrogatories, Defendant has not shown why the case warrants answering more than the 78 interrogatories already propounded. As to the Fourth Set of Requests for Production: these are duplicative of the third set of RFPs, and are denied for the same reason set forth above. As to the Third Set of Requests for Admission, Defendant has not met his burden of justifying an additional 88 requests for admission. Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA ("National") moves to compel further responses to Interrogatories 1-3, 5-11, 13-29, and 33-47. The Judge Pro Tem rejects Plaintiff's argument that National failed to adequately meet and confer. Turning to the merits, as to the Interrogatories that ask for the identification of documents, Nos. 3, 9, 15, 18, 21, 24, 27, 35, 38, 41, 44, and 47, the tentative ruling is to GRANT the motion. As to the Interrogatories that seek the identification of witnesses, Nos. 2, 8, 14, 17, 20, 23, 26, 34, 37, 40, and 43, the tentative ruling is to DENY the motion. As to the Interrogatories that seek the identification of facts, the tentative ruling is to GRANT the motion as to Interrogatory Nos. 1, 5, 6, 7, 33, 36, 39, 42, 45, and 46 and DENY the motion as to Interrogatory Nos. 10, 11, 13, 16, 19, 22, 25, 28 and 29. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: REAL ESTATE EBROKER, INC. VS. EDWARD LITKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Deft And For Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF REAL ESTATE EBROKER, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel the deposition of Defendant Edward Litke is denied as moot. On June 18, 2013 - two days before Plaintiff filed this motion -- the parties agreed on potential dates in August for Mr. Litke's deposition, subject to obtaining a continuance of the July 29 trial date. On June 25, the Court continued the trial date to September 16. Accordingly, the deposition can now proceed on the agreed upon August dates, and this motion is, thus, moot. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA PACE VS. TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Mtion To Stay Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 15, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE Motion To Stay Arbitration. Denied. Respondent has failed to show that this Court has jurisdiction to decide this motion, or even that there is any basis to do so at this time. This is not a discovery motion pursuant to CCP 2016.010, nor does Respondent identify any relief sought other than a stay. By contrast, there is a pending discovery motion set for hearing before the Court on July 22 regarding the testimony of Mr. Paez which could moot some or all of this motion. Further, the criminal trial of Mr. Mora is in less than a month. Respondent has not even stated when the arbitration is scheduled to begin. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
